subject: Re: Performance Issue for Exporting Subtomo
body:
Hi Wang,
Warp was originally built with the expectation of having ~16GB GPU memory available, unfortunately your card isn’t big enough and that’s where your issues are coming from.
Please note that desktop warp is unsupported right now, we have a Linux version with a command line interface 
warpem.com
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Apr 8, 2025, at 01:27, Wang GuangLei <a824668867@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿
Hi everyone,


I’m currently using Warp for Windows to export subtomo particles, and I’ve
run into a few issues I hope you can help with. The export speed seems
unusually slow and varies significantly, ranging from 30 minutes to 16 hours
for a single tomogram. All of my tomograms have the same size and the same
number of particles, so I’m not sure why the exporting time are different.


To provide more context, I’m using a single 1080Ti (11GB) GPU to export 3000 128×128×128 particles from 4096×4096×1000 tomograms in WARP 1.0.9. Is it normal for this process to take over 10 hours to complete?


Additionally, I’ve noticed that some of tomograms may produce  completely black (all pixels are zero) subtomos,
and lower exporting speed will produce more black subtomos. This leads me to
wonder if this performance is typical for exporting in Warp. At this rate, it
looks like I’d need about 60 days to export all of my data.


I also observed the following warnings in the console during the export process:


```


Received “TomoExport Particles” with 4 arguments, for GPU
#0, 10188 MB free.  


ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag
65001(0xfde9) encountered.  


ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag
65002(0xfdea) encountered.  


ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag
65001(0xfde9) encountered.  


ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag
65002(0xfdea) encountered.  


```


Additionally, I’ve noticed that my GPU memory seems to be full, and there
is an additional 20GB of shared memory in use. The memory clock is running at
2400 MHz, which is considerably lower than the GPU memory speed. Could this be the
case?


Any advice or suggestions would be greatly appreciated! Thanks in advance
for your help.


Best wishes
Wang GuangLei
